puzzle.title: The Voice of Concealed Conviction
puzzle.intro: In the heart of a tyrannical court, where disbelief was the law of the land, a lone soul walked a tightrope of faith and fear. This individual, close to the center of power, listened as a prophet's message was met with scorn and threats. A moment of truth arrived, forcing this person to choose between silent safety and speaking a dangerous truth. Can you uncover the identity of this courageous advocate?
clues: I lived within the very household of a tyrant, witnessing firsthand the arrogance that rejected divine signs.
I kept my faith a secret, a hidden treasure in a palace of idolatry.
I heard the leader I served plot to kill a messenger of Allah, a man named Musa.
I questioned their logic, reminding them of the fate of the people of Nuh, 'Ad, and Thamud.
I posed a critical question to my people: 'If he is a liar, his lie is upon him; but if he is truthful, some of what he promises will strike you.'
My story is told as an example of faith in the surah named 'The Forgiver,' where I warned my people against the schemes of the powerful.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Believer from Fir'awn's Family
Haman
The Magicians of Fir'awn
Qarun
The Samiri

trial.label: What Was My Trial?
options: Defending Prophet Musa in the tyrant's court
Building a lofty tower to reach the heavens
Being cast into a blazing fire
Being swallowed by a giant fish
Losing all wealth and health
trial.answer: Defending Prophet Musa in the tyrant's court

location.label: What Was My Argument's Theme?
options: The consequence of rejecting a prophet's message
The demand for a sign from a sacred tree
The creation of a calf from molten jewelry
The miracle of a camel from rock
The interpretation of a ruler's dreams
location.answer: The consequence of rejecting a prophet's message

outcome.label: What Was the Outcome?
options: Allah saved this individual from their evil plot
The entire court immediately accepted Islam
The tyrant repented at the moment of drowning
A great flood destroyed the disbelievers
The individual was exiled to a distant land
outcome.answer: Allah saved this individual from their evil plot

puzzle.arabic: فَوَقَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِ مَا مَكَرُوا۟ ۖ وَحَاقَ بِـَٔالِ فِرْعَوْنَ سُوٓءُ ٱلْعَذَابِ
puzzle.verse: So Allāh protected him from the evils they plotted, and the people of Pharaoh were enveloped by the worst of punishment - (40:45)
